macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin and Ethereum rebound as DLC surges 103%
items.summary: Bitcoin rose 1.9% to about $62,848 while Ethereum gained 3.8% to around $1,660, signaling a broader crypto market recovery. Diverge Loop (DLC) was the standout mover, jumping 103.4% in 24 hours.

items.title: Crypto liquidations top $546 million in 24 hours
items.summary: Perpetual futures markets saw more than $546 million liquidated over the past day, led by Bitcoin and Ethereum. The liquidation mix was dominated by short positions, indicating a sharp short squeeze.

items.title: Bitcoin rebounds above $63,000 amid persistent ETF outflows
items.summary: Bitcoin recovered above the $63,000 level after defending key support, while Ethereum gained more than 5% in the same period. Market sentiment remained cautious as spot Bitcoin ETFs continued to see heavy net outflows.

items.title: Bitcoin, Ethereum, XRP and Dogecoin spring back as market volatility continues
items.summary: Major cryptocurrencies rebounded even as geopolitical tensions in the Middle East added pressure to risk assets. The report also noted more than $660 million in market liquidations over 24 hours and extreme fear in crypto sentiment.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Anthropic says AI is accelerating software development inside the company
items.summary: Anthropic published new analysis claiming its own engineers now ship about 8x as much code per quarter as they did from 2021–2025, and that Claude models can now handle tasks lasting up to about 12 hours. The post argues that AI systems are improving at longer-horizon work, with open-ended task success and experiment-proposal capabilities rising sharply in 2026.

items.title: New benchmark data suggests AI agents still struggle with real knowledge work
items.summary: Commentary discussing the new Agent’s Last Exam benchmark says tested models averaged only a 2.6% pass rate, highlighting major limits in agentic performance on fluid knowledge-work tasks. The same discussion notes weak results on ARC-AGI-style interactive reasoning benchmarks, reinforcing skepticism about near-term AGI claims.

items.title: Industry debate intensifies over whether AI is boosting productivity or adding 'slop'
items.summary: A recent AI industry discussion frames a growing split between claims of rapid productivity gains and concerns that much of the output is low-value 'AI slop.' The broader trend highlighted is that attention-heavy AI content may be expanding supply without proportional economic growth.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.
